text: The surgeon uses an electrocautery device and a harmonic scalpel to carefully dissect through visible adhesions and scar tissue, clearing the surgical field.
The surgeon follows the tube to locate the gastric band, preparing to open the area to access the stomach for band removal.

The surgeon identifies the buckle of the gastric band embedded within the stomach wall. The band appears dark brown due to exposure to stomach acid. The surgeon prepares to cut the buckle loose for removal.
The surgeon introduces a suction device into the surgical field in anticipation of stomach content, preparing to manage any fluid or debris during the gastric band removal procedure.
The surgeon stitches up the large hole left in the stomach wall after the removal of the gastric band, using sutures to close the defect.

The surgeon has closed the hole in the stomach with sutures. The contaminated band is placed inside a sterile bag to prevent contact with the abdominal wall and incision, and is then removed from the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon places a drain in the abdominal cavity as a precautionary measure following the removal of the gastric band.
